<output>
Here is the full report of the project at `/home/user/workspace`.

---

## 1. Next.js Version

**`next: "16.2.0-canary.41"`** -- This is a canary (pre-release) build of Next.js 16, which is notable since Next.js 16 has not been released as stable yet (as of mid-2026).

## 2. Full Directory Structure (excluding node_modules)

```
/home/user/workspace/
  .git/
  .gitignore
  components/
    AppProvider.js
  next.config.ts
  pages/
    404.js
    _app.js
    _document.js
    _error.js
    index.js
    api/
      posts/
        [id].js
        index.js
    blog/
      [id].js
      index.js
  styles/
    globals.css
  tsconfig.json
  package.json
  package-lock.json
```

## 3. All Files in `pages/` (Recursively, with Content Summaries)

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/index.js`
Homepage -- uses `getServerSideProps`. Fetches 5 posts from JSONPlaceholder. Passes `userAgent` and `timestamp` as props. Renders a welcome message, the timestamp, user agent string, a list of 5 post links, and a "View All Posts" button using `router.push('/blog')`.

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/_app.js`
Custom App component -- wraps all pages in `AppProvider` context. Renders a global layout with a `<header>` (site title, Home/Blog nav links via `next/link`), `<main>` for page content, and `<footer>`. Imports global CSS from `../styles/globals.css`.

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/_document.js`
Custom Document -- sets `<Html lang="en">`. Includes meta description, favicon link, Google Fonts preconnect, and Inter font stylesheet. Renders `<Main />` and `<NextScript />`.

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/_error.js`
Custom error page -- uses `getInitialProps` to extract `statusCode` from `res` or `err`. Displays server-side vs client-side error messaging, with special 404 handling ("This page could not be found." vs generic "Sorry, something went wrong.").

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/404.js`
Custom 404 page -- independent of `_error.js`. Displays "404 - Page Not Found" with a meta description and a "Go Back Home" button using `router.push('/')`.

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/blog/index.js`
Blog listing page (SSG + ISR) -- uses `getStaticProps` with `revalidate: 60`. Fetches all posts from JSONPlaceholder. Renders a grid of post cards (title, 100-char excerpt), each with a "Read More" button using `router.push`.

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/blog/[id].js`
Single blog post page (SSG + ISR) -- uses `getStaticPaths` (first 10 posts, `fallback: 'blocking'`) and `getStaticProps` (revalidate 300). Fetches post + comments from JSONPlaceholder in parallel. Handles `fallback` with a loading div. Renders post title/body, a back button, and a comments section. Error path returns `{ notFound: true }`.

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/api/posts/index.js`
API route -- handles `GET` (returns simulated array of 2 posts) and `POST` (validates title/content required, returns simulated created post). Returns 405 for other methods.

### `/home/user/workspace/pages/api/posts/[id].js`
API route with dynamic param -- handles `GET` (returns simulated single post), `PUT` (returns simulated updated post), and `DELETE` (returns success message). Returns 405 for other methods.

## 4. `tsconfig.json` Configuration

```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2017",
    "lib": ["dom", "dom.iterable", "esnext"],
    "allowJs": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "module": "esnext",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "incremental": true,
    "plugins": [{ "name": "next" }],
    "paths": { "@/*": ["./*"] }
  },
  "include": ["next-env.d.ts", "**/*.ts", "**/*.tsx", ".next/types/**/*.ts"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules"]
}
```

Key points: `strict: true`, `moduleResolution: "bundler"`, `jsx: "react-jsx"`, uses the Next.js TS plugin, and the `@/*` path alias maps to the project root.

## 5. `app/` Directory

**Does not exist.** This project uses the Pages Router exclusively. There is no `app/` directory at all.

## 6. Layout and Middleware Files

**No layout files or middleware files found.** There are:
- No `middleware.ts` or `middleware.js` anywhere in the project.
- No explicit `layout.js`/`layout.tsx` files. However, `/home/user/workspace/pages/_app.js` serves as the global layout wrapper (standard for Pages Router), providing a shared header/nav/footer shell via `AppProvider`.

## 7. Supporting Files

### `/home/user/workspace/next.config.ts`
Minimal config -- empty `NextConfig` object (`{}`) exported. No custom webpack, redirects, rewrites, or images configuration.

### `/home/user/workspace/components/AppProvider.js`
React context provider for a `theme` state (`'light'` default). Exports `useAppContext` hook that throws if used outside the provider.

### `/home/user/workspace/styles/globals.css`
Global stylesheet -- CSS reset (`box-sizing`, `margin`, `padding` zeroed). Inter font family. Styles for: `.app-container` (1200px max-width), header/nav, `.posts-grid` (responsive grid with `auto-fit`/`minmax(300px, 1fr)`), `.post-card`, `.comments`/`.comment`, `.error-page`, and `button` (blue #0070f3 theme).

### Dependencies Summary
| Package | Version |
|---|---|
| `next` | `16.2.0-canary.41` |
| `react` / `react-dom` | `19.1.0` |
| `ai` | `5.0.59` |
| `@ai-sdk/react` | `2.0.0-beta.28` |
| `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` | `^1.17.0` |
| `zod` | `^4.0.10` |
| `typescript` | `^5` |
| `vitest` | `^3.1.3` |

text:
Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the project. Let me read the full content of all the files I need to migrate so I can make accurate conversions.

Key points:
- Next.js 16.2.0-canary.41 (very recent canary)
- All files are currently `.js` (JavaScript, not TypeScript)
- The user wants TypeScript types added
- App Router doesn't exist yet — needs to be created
- Pages Router files need to be migrated to App Router equivalents

Let me read all the source files to understand their exact content before creating the App Router versions.

Here's my migration plan:

### Pages Router → App Router Mapping

1. **`pages/_app.js`** → `app/layout.tsx` (root layout)
2. **`pages/_document.js`** → Handled by `app/layout.tsx` (metadata API replaces `<Head>`, `<Html>`, `<Body>`)
3. **`pages/index.js`** → `app/page.tsx` (homepage)
4. **`pages/404.js`** → `app/not-found.tsx`
5. **`pages/_error.js`** → `app/error.tsx` (but App Router has a different approach - error boundaries)
6. **`pages/blog/index.js`** → `app/blog/page.tsx`
7. **`pages/blog/[id].js`** → `app/blog/[id]/page.tsx`
8. **`pages/api/posts/index.js`** → `app/api/posts/route.ts`
9. **`pages/api/posts/[id].js`** → `app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts`

### API Conversions:
- `getServerSideProps` → use `async` component (fetch directly in the component) or use `unstable_noStore` + fetch
- `getStaticProps` → use `fetch` with `next: { revalidate }` or `generateStaticParams`
- `getStaticPaths` → `generateStaticParams`
- `getInitialProps` → App Router doesn't support this; need error boundary pattern
- `useRouter()` for `router.push()` → `useRouter()` from `next/navigation` (different import)
- `next/link` usage stays the same
- `next/head` → metadata API (export `metadata` object)
- API routes: Route Handlers use `NextRequest`/`NextResponse` from `next/server`

Let me read all the files first.
Now let me read all the source files to understand their exact content.

text: Now create the error boundary. In App Router, `error.tsx` is a Client Component that receives `error` and `reset` props.

output: Updated task #5 status
Updated task #6 status

text:
For the error.tsx, in App Router:
- It must be a Client Component (`'use client'`)
- It receives `error` and `reset` as props
- `error` has a `digest` property (for server-side errors)
- `error.message` contains the error message
- `reset()` allows retrying

The old `_error.js` had:
- Server-side vs client-side error distinction
- Status code display (404 vs generic)

In App Router, 404s are handled by `not-found.tsx`, and all other errors by `error.tsx`. So I don't need the `statusCode === 404` check since that path is now handled by `not-found.tsx`.

However, I should make the error page informative. Let me create it.

For the back button, I'll create a small inline client component.

Let me also think about `params` — in Next.js 15+, `params` is a Promise.

text:
Now let me create the API route handlers. App Router uses a different pattern:
- Export named functions `GET`, `POST`, `PUT`, `DELETE` instead of a single `handler`
- Use `NextRequest` and `NextResponse`
- Unsupported methods automatically return 405

For validation pattern: In Pages Router, `res.status(400).json({ error })`, in App Router, return `NextResponse.json({ error }, { status: 400 })`.

For `req.body` in Pages Router, it was parsed by body parser middleware. In App Router, `req.json()` parses the body.

For `req.query` in Pages Router, `id` came from `req.query`. In App Router, params are passed as the second argument.

Let me create both API route handlers.

### Route mapping

| Pages Router | App Router | Strategy |
|---|---|---|
| `pages/_app.js` | `app/layout.tsx` | Root layout with AppProvider, header/nav/footer |
| `pages/_document.js` | `app/layout.tsx` | Metadata API + `next/font/google` (Inter) |
| `pages/index.js` | `app/page.tsx` | Server Component with `cache: 'no-store'` + `headers()` |
| `pages/404.js` | `app/not-found.tsx` | Server Component with `<Link>` |
| `pages/_error.js` | `app/error.tsx` | Client Component error boundary (`'use client'`) |
| `pages/blog/index.js` | `app/blog/page.tsx` | Server Component with `next: { revalidate: 60 }` |
| `pages/blog/[id].js` | `app/blog/[id]/page.tsx` | `generateStaticParams` + `generateMetadata` + ISR |
| — | `app/blog/[id]/loading.tsx` | Loading state (replaces `router.isFallback`) |
| — | `app/blog/[id]/BackButton.tsx` | Client component for `router.back()` |
| `pages/api/posts/index.js` | `app/api/posts/route.ts` | Named `GET`/`POST` exports with `NextResponse` |
| `pages/api/posts/[id].js` | `app/api/posts/[id]/route.ts` | Named `GET`/`PUT`/`DELETE` with `params` |

### Key API replacements

| Old API | New API |
|---|---|
| `getServerSideProps` | Async Server Component + `fetch(…, {cache:'no-store'})` |
| `getStaticProps` + `revalidate` | Server Component + `fetch(…, {next:{revalidate:N}})` |
| `getStaticPaths` + `fallback` | `generateStaticParams` (blocking is default) |
| `getInitialProps` | `error.tsx` (`error`, `reset` props) |
| `next/head` | `metadata` / `generateMetadata` export |
| `next/document` (`Html`, `Head`, `Main`, `NextScript`) | Handled by root layout + metadata API |
| `useRouter` from `next/router` | `useRouter` from `next/navigation` (or `<Link>` in SC) |
| `router.push()` | `<Link href="…">` |
| `req.headers` in gSSP | `headers()` from `next/headers` |
| API `handler(req, res)` | Named `GET`/`POST`/`PUT`/`DELETE` with `NextResponse` |
| `res.status(405).end(…)` | Auto-405 for unexported methods in route handlers |
